title: Redirects in federated SPARQL queries
description:
Hi,
I notice that Virtuoso (7.2.0.1) does not follow HTTP redirects (301) when federating SPARQL queries using the SERVICE keyword. The UniProt endpoint has just come out of beta and consequently changed URL, from http://beta.sparql.uniprot.org to http://sparql.uniprot.org. A redirect is in place but Virtuoso does not follow it, instead giving an error:
Virtuoso RDFZZ Error DB.DBA.SPARQL_REXEC('http://beta.sparql.uniprot.org/sparql', ...) returned Content-Type 'text/html' status 'HTTP/1.0 301 Moved Permanently



301 Moved Permanently

Moved Permanently
The document has moved here.



SPARQL query:
define sql:big-data-const 0
#output-format:text/html
PREFIX rdf:
PREFIX rdfs:
PREFIX owl:
PREFIX dcterms:
PREFIX obo:
PREFIX sio:
PREFIX efo:
PREFIX atlas:
PREFIX atlasterms:

PREFIX upc:
PREFIX identifiers:

SELECT distinct ?valueLabel ?goid ?golabel
WHERE {
?value rdfs:label ?valueLabel .
?value atlasterms:isMeasurementOf ?probe  .
?probe atlasterms:dbXref identifiers:ENSG00000129991  .
?probe atlasterms:dbXref ?uniprot .
SERVICE  {
?uniprot a upc:Protein  .
?uniprot upc:classifiedWith ?keyword  .
?keyword rdfs:seeAlso ?goid  .
?goid rdfs:label ?golabel  .
}
}

Is this expected behaviour. Would it be different for a temporary redirect (302)?
Cheers,
Andy
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE proteins (id INT PRIMARY KEY, name VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL, sequence TEXT, organism VARCHAR(255), created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP);
INSERT INTO proteins (id, name, sequence, organism) VALUES (1, 'ProteinA', 'MKTAYIAKQRQISFVKSHFSRQD', 'Homo sapiens');
INSERT INTO proteins (id, name, sequence, organism) VALUES (2, 'ProteinB', NULL, 'Mus musculus');
SELECT p.name, p.sequence FROM proteins p WHERE p.sequence IS NOT NULL AND LENGTH(p.sequence) > 10;
SELECT p1.name, p2.name FROM proteins p1 JOIN proteins p2 ON p1.id <> p2.id WHERE p1.organism = p2.organism;
key_bug_features: Virtuoso does not follow HTTP redirects in SPARQL queries
